title: 10 best herbal capsules for gum pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The herb contains sesquiterpenes, compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When taken as a supplement, sage capsules can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, providing relief from conditions such as gum disease, gingivitis, and receding gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Salvianolic acids: Sage's salvianolic acid content has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anxiolytic effects, helping to alleviate gum pain and discomfort while also reducing anxiety and stress.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and prevent tissue damage, which contributes to gum pain relief.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infections and promoting healing in the gums.
Salvianolic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Salvia officinalis for making capsules due to their high concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds.
Seeds: Salvia officinalis seeds are used to make capsules for gum pain relief due to their richness in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Salvia officinalis roots are used to make capsules for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the flowers are in the bud stage.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or a clean cloth for 2-3 days.
Grind 10-15 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetarian capsules in a ratio of 1:5.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with gum pain because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated gums. The capsules often combine herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and sage, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort. As the herbal compounds are released into the bloodstream, they target specific areas of the mouth, providing rapid relief from gum inflammation and pain. Regular consumption of these capsules can also promote healthy gums by reducing bacterial growth and promoting a balanced oral microbiome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas such as chamomile, peppermint, and ginger contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from gum pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Certain herbal teas like tea tree oil, eucalyptus, and sage have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, which can cause gum pain and inflammation.
Astringent properties: Herbal teas like myrrh, goldenseal, and sage contain astringent compounds that help reduce bleeding and shrink swollen tissues, providing relief from gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins are a type of flavonoid that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat gum infections and reduce pain.
Theanine: This amino acid has a calming effect, reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to gum pain and inflammation, and promoting relaxation and healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves are commonly used to make capsules for gum pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Roots: Camellia sinensis roots are used for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections and promote healing in gum pain.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality camellia sinensis leaves from a reputable supplier.
Clean and dry the camellia sinensis leaves using a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried camellia sinensis leaves into a fine powder using.
Mix the powdered camellia sinensis with a natural filler such as.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the camellia sinensis mixture using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, providing relief from swelling, redness, and discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm's antibacterial properties help combat bad breath and prevent infection, promoting a healthy oral environment that minimizes gum pain and promotes overall dental well-being. As a result, herbal lemon balm capsules can provide natural and effective relief from gum pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to increased sensitivity and pain in the teeth and gums.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Limonene: A terpene found in lemon balm, limonene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
Borneol: A monoterpene ketone with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, borneol can help numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe gum pain and inflammation.
Buds: They are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce gum pain and prevent infections.
Leaves: They are used again due to their high content of melissic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe gum pain and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers when in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 40-50 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Fill empty capsules with 200-300 milligrams of the powder and seal them using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with gum pain because they contain flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The capsules also possess antioxidant compounds that combat free radicals, which can damage gum tissue and exacerbate pain. Additionally, maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to treat oral infections and inflammation, making it a natural and effective remedy for alleviating gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to gum pain.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The herbal extracts in Maidenhair Tree capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums associated with periodontal disease or gingivitis.

Vasodilatory Effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may improve circulation to the gums, which can help reduce gum pain and inflammation by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with gum conditions.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Bilobalide helps reduce inflammation and has antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the gums from further damage.

Ginkgo Flavone Glycosides (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavone glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce gum pain and inflammation, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for gum pain relief due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for gum pain relief due to their flavonoid content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for gum pain relief due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaf powder containing 24% flavonoids on a digital scale.
Mix the powder with 250 grams of rice flour in a large bowl to create a uniform blend.
Add 10 grams of microcrystalline cellulose as a filler to the bowl and stir well.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the blend per capsule.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia capsules helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as paraguayan stevia, contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. When taken as a supplement, these compounds can help to alleviate symptoms of gum disease such as tenderness and sensitivity, allowing for improved oral health and reduced pain. Additionally, stevia's antibacterial properties can also help to combat the underlying causes of gum pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Stevia's antioxidant compounds help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to gum pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The flavonoids present in Stevia have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain caused by inflammatory conditions such as gingivitis or periodontitis.

Soothing and Calming Effects: Stevia's compounds, particularly stevioside and rebaudioside A, have been shown to exhibit a calming effect on the mucous membranes, which can help to reduce gum inflammation, bleeding, and pain.
remedy_constituents: Steviol Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Quercetin: This flavonoid antioxidant has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infections and soothe gum pain.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in gum tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Stevia rebaudiana leaves are widely used due to their high concentration of steviol glycosides, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stevia rebaudiana stems are used for their steviol glycosides content, which helps in reducing gum pain and inflammation.
Roots: Stevia rebaudiana roots are also used for their high steviol glycosides content, which provides relief from gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves in their peak potency during the morning hours after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested stevia rebaudiana leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Extract the bioactive compounds from dried stevia rebaudiana leaves using 95 percent ethanol in a ratio of 1:2 for 30 minutes.
Filter the stevia rebaudiana ethanol extract using a whatman filter paper to remove any impurities instantly.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of stevia rebaudiana extract and seal them using a capsule filling machine immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo capsules helps with gum pain because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. The herb Pelargonium sidoides, in particular, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe gum tissue and reduce pain associated with conditions such as periodontitis and gingivitis. Additionally, Umckaloabo's antibacterial properties may also help combat underlying infections that contribute to gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Umckaloabo's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's antimicrobial agents, including terpenes and saponins, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, reducing the risk of abscesses and other complications.
Wound healing promotion: Umckaloabo's bioactive compounds stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound strength, facilitating faster healing of gum lesions, ulcers, or other oral wounds.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation causing pain.
Geraniol: This monoterpenoid has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce bacterial growth in the mouth and prevent infections that can cause gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for gum health.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
Stems: They are used for their potential antimicrobial properties, which can help fight gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of pelargonium sidoides plants after 3-4 years of cultivation in a controlled environment.
Dry the harvested roots in a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Crush 30 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of cellulose powder in a ratio of 1:3 to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder blend and seal the capsules using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with gum pain because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the gums and alleviate discomfort. The herbal extract also contains antioxidants that combat free radicals, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further inflammation. Additionally, dandelion capsules may help to neutralize acid levels in the mouth, reducing the risk of tooth decay and sensitivity. As a result, many people find relief from chronic gum pain and inflammation by incorporating dandelion capsules into their oral health routine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which can reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in dandelion capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to gum pain and inflammation.

Vasodilatory effects: Dandelion root's vasodilatory compounds can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which may reduce gum pain by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the affected areas.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help with gum pain by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the affected area.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, found in Taraxacum officinale, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate gum pain and promote healing.
Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale may help with gum pain by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting the growth of healthy gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum pain.

Roots: They are used because of their rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and pain.
Flowers: They are used due to their content of flavonoids and other compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and protect the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves at the optimal time to ensure potency and effectiveness in capsules.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 15 minutes.
Mix 200 milligrams of the ground taraxacum officinale powder with 100 milligrams of starch and 10 milligrams of silica in a ratio of 3:1:1.
Fill 100-capsule capsules with the taraxacum officinale powder mixture using a capsule filling machine at 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese snakegourd capsules helps with gum pain because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that target the root cause of gum discomfort. The capsules' active ingredients, such as cucurbitacins and triterpenoids, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate inflammation in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for managing gum pain caused by gingivitis, periodontal disease, or tooth extractions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The capsules contain compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain in the gums caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in the capsules help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of gum disease.
Analgesic: The capsules contain analgesic compounds that help relieve pain and discomfort in the gums, providing rapid and effective relief from gum pain and sensitivity.
remedy_constituents: Cucurbitacins: These triterpenoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce gum pain and swelling.
Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol, isorhapontigenin): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate gum pain and promote healing.
Alkaloids (e.g., berberine): These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat gum infections and reduce pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help in relieving pain and swelling in the gums.
Seeds: They are used as they contain trichosanthin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making it useful for gum pain.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help in treating gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of the plant trichosanthes kirilowii, at the optimal time, usually in autumn, for maximum potency.
Dry the roots using a food dehydrator or by air-drying them, for a minimum of 3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or a mortar and pestle, for 2-3 minutes.
Mix the powdered roots with a binding agent like gum arabic, at a ratio of 1:1, to help hold the capsule contents together.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered root mixture, using a spoon or a capsule filler, for 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with gum pain because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, work synergistically to reduce inflammation in the gums, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with conditions like periodontitis and gingivitis. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort capsules have been shown to improve blood circulation, which helps to promote healing and regeneration of gum tissue, further reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsule's active compounds, such as centellosides and triterpenoids, reduce inflammation in the gums, which helps to alleviate swelling and pain associated with gum disease.

Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort contains high levels of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting gum tissue from damage and promoting healing.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: The capsule's constituents stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood circulation, and exhibit antibacterial activity, all of which contribute to the acceleration of wound healing, reduction of bacterial load, and alleviation of pain associated with gum inflammation and infection.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, leading to relief from gum pain.
Asiatic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum conditions.
Madecassoside: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce gum inflammation and promote healing in gum tissues, leading to relief from gum pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate gum pain.
Stems: Utilized for their triterpenoid saponins, which have similar effects to the leaves and contribute to the overall pain-relieving properties of Centella asiatica capsules.
Roots: Employed for their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Weigh 2 ounces of the dried centella asiatica and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the centella asiatica mixture using a capsule filler and store them in an airtight container.
Label the capsules with the date and the contents and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark capsules helps with gum pain because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called periplocin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the gums. Additionally, the capsules' flavonoids and phenolic acids work synergistically to promote healthy gum tissue and prevent further damage. By addressing the underlying causes of gum pain, Jesuit's bark capsules provide a safe and natural solution for soothing irritated gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Salicin reduces swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.
Pain relief: Salicin's analgesic properties help alleviate mild to moderate pain, including gum pain, by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.
Antibacterial: Willow bark has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which may help combat infections and reduce the risk of gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Quinidine: As an alkaloid, quinidine has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort in the gums.
Cinchonine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat gum infections that may be contributing to pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of quinine and other alkaloids, which are used to treat gum pain.
Barks: The barks are used as a source of quinine and other alkaloids, which are extracted and used to treat gum pain.
Stems: The stems, like the barks, are also a source of quinine and other alkaloids, making them useful in treating gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona pubescens bark in early spring or late summer when the tree is about 5-10 years old.
Dry the harvested bark for 2-3 weeks in a warm and well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin or vegetable cellulose to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains powerful compounds that reduce swelling and soothe irritated gums, providing relief from discomfort and sensitivity. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and plaque buildup, which can cause gum inflammation and pain. By promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing inflammation, herbal aloe capsules offer a natural solution for alleviating gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in gums, providing relief from inflammation-related discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal agents present in herbal aloe capsules help combat gum infections and prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms, promoting healthy oral tissues.

Wound healing activity: Herbal aloe capsules contain compounds that accelerate wound healing, improving the regeneration of damaged gum tissue and reducing the risk of infection or further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe leaves are commonly used to reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain.
Roots: Containing bioactive compounds that have analgesic properties, aloe roots help to numb the gums and provide relief from pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic aloe vera gel, vitamin c powder, and gelatin capsules.
Mix one teaspoon of aloe vera gel with two hundred milligrams of vitamin c powder.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for twenty minutes or until it thickens slightly.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled aloe vera-vitamin c mixture and seal.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated gums. The menthol in peppermint capsules numbs the affected area, reducing discomfort and sensitivity. Additionally, peppermint's antiseptic properties help combat bacteria that can cause inflammation, promoting healthy gum tissue and a reduction in pain. Regular use of peppermint capsules can provide long-term relief from gum pain, making them a natural and effective remedy for a range of oral health issues.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a major component of peppermint oil, helps to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from gum pain and inflammation by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is another key compound in peppermint oil that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and proteins.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in peppermint capsules, such as rosmarinic acid and hesperidin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect the gums from further damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a significant amount of menthone, a compound that helps to numb the gums and provide relief from pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a well-maintained garden or purchase dried peppermint herb in bulk measuring out two ounces per serving.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a dehydrator set at 105 degrees fahrenheit for four hours or air-drying them for seven days.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for one minute.
Mix the ground peppermint powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer over low heat for twenty-five minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the cooled peppermint extract mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in blueberries, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with gum pain. Additionally, the capsules' natural ability to stimulate blood flow may help to reduce swelling and promote healing. By taking herbal blueberry capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from gum pain caused by various factors, including gingivitis, tooth sensitivity, and even dental work.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants found in blueberries, help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress.
Fiber: The fiber content in blueberry capsules helps to maintain healthy gums by reducing plaque formation and preventing bacterial buildup, which can contribute to gum pain and discomfort.

Polyphenols: Polyphenols, a type of antioxidant found in blueberries, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat oral infections and inflammation, making them effective in alleviating gum pain.
Barks: Blueberry barks have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections associated with gum disease and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently washing it with cold running water to remove dirt particles.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours to maintain its potency.
Grind 250 grams of dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin to create capsules that can be stored for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhubarb capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as anthraquinones, work to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, providing relief from painful conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis. Additionally, rhubarb has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, which are often linked to oral health problems. By addressing both internal and external factors, rhubarb capsules can help alleviate gum pain and promote a healthy mouth.
remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: Rhubarb's astringent compounds help to reduce inflammation and bleeding in the gums, which can alleviate gum pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontitis.
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Rhubarb contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.
Stomachic Properties: Rhubarb's stomachic properties can help to soothe digestive issues like heartburn and indigestion, which are often linked to gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rhubarb roots contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe gum pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhizomes of the rhubarb plant are rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that coats and protects the mouth tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately two hundred grams of dried rheum officinale root and place it in a clean glass container.
Combine one hundred milliliters of vodka as a solvent with the dried root mixture and let it steep for twenty-four hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids, retaining two hundred milliliters of the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with three hundred grams of gelatin capsules and fill them to desired dosage, taking approximately five minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation, requiring no further preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules help with gum pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the body. When taken as a supplement, St John's Wort capsules may help alleviate gum pain caused by conditions such as gingivitis, periodontitis, or toothaches. By reducing swelling and discomfort, these capsules can promote relaxation and relief from gum pain, allowing for improved oral health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort, such as hyperoside and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a naphthodianthrone compound that has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing gum pain and discomfort.
Tannins: Tannins in St John's Wort have astringent properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation by contracting the mucous membranes and reducing bleeding, thereby alleviating pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: St John's Wort roots have been traditionally used to alleviate pain and inflammation, making them a suitable ingredient for medicated capsules to soothe gum pain.
Bark: The bark of the St John's Wort plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce gum swelling and discomfort.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them an effective ingredient for treating gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried st john's wort herb and fill a clean glass jar with one cup of the material.
Measure out two tablespoons of dried st john's wort herb and set aside for future use.
Combine the remaining herb with 250ml of high-proof vodka in a dark glass bottle.
Store the mixture in a cool place for four weeks, shaking the bottle daily for twenty minutes.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and fill capsules with the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The capsule's active compound, thujone, targets the root cause of gum inflammation and infection, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, wormwood's natural astringent properties help to tighten and soothe inflamed gum tissue, promoting a healthier oral environment. By addressing the underlying causes of gum pain, herbal wormwood capsules provide effective relief from chronic gum issues, allowing for improved overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Thujone: Thujone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce gum pain by numbing the affected area and reducing swelling.
Camphor: Camphor is a natural antiseptic that helps to combat bacterial infections in the gums, reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with gum disease.

Essential oils: Wormwood essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and calm gum tissue, reducing pain and discomfort caused by gum inflammation or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving gum pain.
Barks: Containing high amounts of thujone, a natural anesthetic that can help numb the gums and reduce discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the morning when they are dry and crumbly, ensuring an adequate quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method at 35°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, measuring out 250 milligrams of powder per capsule.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered artemisia absinthium and seal them securely, preparing a batch of 100 capsules for future use.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight, allowing them to sit for 24 hours before consumption to allow flavors to meld.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The capsule's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to ease inflammation, while also promoting blood flow and oxygenation to the affected area. This natural remedy also contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals, reducing gum irritation and promoting a healthy oral environment, leading to reduced pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow capsules contain compounds like achillein, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum pain.
Antioxidant: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect gums from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the gums.
Stimulates blood flow: The capillary-acting compounds in yarrow capsules improve blood circulation to the gums, which helps to reduce bleeding, pain, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum pain.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called coumarin, which has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing of the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers at dawn or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Grind 30g of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 100mg of gelatin capsules and fill each capsule with approximately 200mg of the herbal mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, taking 2-3 capsules twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with gum pain because they contain californianum, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and alleviates discomfort. The capsules also have antiseptic properties, which combat bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in pot marigold capsules help protect the gums from further damage by neutralizing free radicals. By targeting the root causes of gum pain, these capsules provide fast and effective relief from toothaches and gum sensitivity.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Pot marigold capsules contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
Vitexin: Vitexin is a flavone found in pot marigold that has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in relieving gum pain and discomfort.

Luteolin: Luteolin, another flavonoid present in pot marigold, has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat gum infections and reduce swelling that may contribute to gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used to make medicinal capsules because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain in the gums, making them an effective remedy for gum pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Dry the flowers using a warm air circulation method or desiccant for at least 24 hours.
Crush the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one part of the powdered calendula with one part of gelatin and add distilled water to form a paste, let it sit for 10 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the paste mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The allantoin present in comfrey has been shown to reduce swelling and promote healing, which can be particularly beneficial for gum inflammation and pain caused by conditions such as gingivitis or periodontal disease. Additionally, comfrey's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis can help repair damaged gum tissue, leading to a reduction in pain and discomfort associated with gum disease.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey contains allantoin, a natural compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth, which can alleviate gum pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontal disease.
Rutoside: Rutoside, a flavonoid present in comfrey, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe gum tissue and reduce pain associated with gum inflammation or irritation.
Tannins: Comfrey capsules contain tannins, which have antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum disease and subsequent pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: Contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, combating bacterial infections in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powdered herb with 100mg of silica gel and fill capsules using a capsule machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gum pain are a natural supplement that helps alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with gum pain, promoting oral health and well-being. The best herbal capsules for gum pain contain a blend of effective ingredients such as Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Salvia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Mentha x piperita, Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cinchona officinalis, and Syzygium aromaticum. If left untreated, gum pain can significantly impact daily life, causing difficulties in eating, speaking, and even affecting self-confidence and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions such as toothaches, mouth sores, and earaches. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and clove oil have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help relieve discomfort and reduce swelling associated with these conditions. Other ailments that may be addressed by these herbal remedies include migraines, joint pain, and menstrual cramps.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with gum pain is a blend of Aloe Vera, Turmeric, and Clove. Aloe Vera soothes and calms the gums, reducing inflammation and pain. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that combat gum infection. Clove oil has antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria that cause gum pain and infection. Taking these capsules in combination can provide relief from gum pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall oral health. It's essential to consult a dentist or healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Iranian Journal of Basic Medical Sciences, herbal capsules can be beneficial for gum pain due to the effectiveness of certain herbal products in reducing gingivitis. The study found that the herbal products, such as pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, have a large body of evidence supporting their effectiveness in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding, as well as inhibiting dental plaque formation. Specifically, the study's results showed that 83% of clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis demonstrated a significant improvement in oral hygiene indices and a reduction in microbial counts.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in these capsules, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. By inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which cause pain and inflammation, Zingiber officinale capsules provide relief from gum pain and discomfort. This natural remedy is also known to ease toothaches and gum inflammation, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution to gum health issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Shogaols: They have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
Volatile oils (mainly Gingerene): These oils have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the gums from inflammation and damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used, as they contain similar active compounds to the rhyzomes, which help in relieving pain and inflammation in the gums.

Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale may also be used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain some of the active compounds found in the rhyzomes and roots, providing additional pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh rhizome roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the rhizome roots for 2 days in a controlled environment with 30% relative humidity.
Grind the dried rhizome roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of starch and 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, the active compound found in these capsules, inhibits the production of enzymes that cause inflammation and pain in the gums. This leads to reduced swelling and tenderness in the affected area. Additionally, curcumin's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting a healthy gum environment and alleviating pain associated with gum disease, gingivitis, and other conditions. Regular use of these capsules may provide long-term relief from gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating discomfort and swelling in the gums.
Demethoxycurcumin: It aids in preventing the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing the risk of gum inflammation and pain.
Beta-Caryophyllene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing gum inflammation and alleviating pain by blocking the action of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the Curcuma longa plant, which contains the highest concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also rich in curcumin and are often used to make capsules for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are used to make capsules for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin and 100g of water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes while stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
Pour the mixture into a capsule machine and fill 100 capsules with the herbal mixture.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis capsules helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The salvenolic acid in Salvia officinalis has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum infections, such as periodontitis. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the herb help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gums, making Salvia officinalis capsules a natural and effective remedy for gum pain and inflammation. Regular use may also strengthen gum tissue.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: It helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and antioxidant activity, which in turn alleviate gum swelling and pain.
Salvianolic acid A: It helps with gum pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, thus reducing gum inflammation and preventing infection.
Oleanolic acid: It helps with gum pain by displaying anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help alleviate gum pain.
Seeds: Salvia officinalis seeds contain compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent infections.

Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe gum pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and stems to ensure accurate measurement.
Crush the dried plant material into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 50 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin for 5 minutes.
Load the mixture into a capsule machine and fill each capsule according to manufacturer instructions.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place to preserve their potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with gum pain because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Echinacea purpurea may help combat gum inflammation, alleviate pain, and soothe irritated tissues. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory properties may boost the body's natural defenses against infections that can cause gum pain, making it a potential natural remedy for gum health issues. Regular use may lead to noticeable improvements in gum health.
remedy_constituents: Alkylphenol glycosides: These compounds help alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can prevent infections that contribute to gum pain.
Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides in Echinacea purpurea help with gum pain by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that mitigate the root causes of gum inflammation.
Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides present in Echinacea purpurea capsules aid in gum pain relief by enhancing the immune system's response to oral infections and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat gum infections and reduce pain.

Roots (again, as it's another part of the root system): The roots also contain compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to promote healing and reduce pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 echinacea purpurea flowers when in full bloom and dry them completely using a food dehydrator or low heat oven.
Grind 2 cups of dried echinacea flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 cup of the powder with 1/2 cup of gelatin and 1/4 cup of distilled water in a bowl.
Fill 50-100 empty gelatin capsules with the echinacea mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal echinacea purpurea capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with gum pain because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The peppermint oil present in these capsules contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which have natural pain-relieving properties. When ingested, these compounds help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gum area. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of peppermint oil help prevent infections that may be contributing to the gum pain. This natural remedy provides a soothing solution for individuals suffering from gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: This monoterpene helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and numbing the affected area, providing temporary relief from discomfort.
Menthol: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum pain by numbing the area and reducing swelling.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent infection, ultimately alleviating gum pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve gum pain.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are also used, as they contain menthol and other essential oils that contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: Mentha x piperita buds are sometimes used, as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other essential oils compared to the leaves, making them a potent remedy for gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours.
Weigh out 1:1 ratio of dried leaves to a carrier powder and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the herbal powder mixture using 500mg capsules and a 0.5mm capsule size.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with gum pain because they contain the essential oil of the Eucalyptus plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus has antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and infections that may be causing the pain. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote healthy gums, making it a popular choice for individuals seeking a non-invasive and holistic solution to gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): It helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent.
Caryophyllene: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat the underlying causes of gum pain, such as bacterial infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to alleviate gum pain.
Barks: Used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from gum pain, thanks to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol and cineole.
Stems: Used for their ability to provide antimicrobial properties, which help to combat gum infections and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry the eucalyptus globulus leaves at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their potency and aroma.
Crush 10 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a mortar and pestle to achieve a fine powder.
Mix 10 grams of the crushed eucalyptus globulus powder with 20 grams of vegetable cellulose powder to create the capsule filling.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 300 mg of the eucalyptus globulus mixture.
Store the herbal eucalyptus globulus capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain their quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with gum pain because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules also help to combat infections and prevent further inflammation, promoting healthy gum tissue. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of the licorice root can help to ease pain and irritation, providing relief from gum pain and inflammation. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for gum health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and exhibiting anti-bacterial properties, which contribute to soothing gum irritations.
Liquiritin: It helps with gum pain by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce gum swelling and prevent tissue damage.
Liquorice flavonoids: They help with gum pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing gum inflammation and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce gum pain and inflammation.
Leaves: They are used to prepare a decoction that helps to reduce gum inflammation, ease pain, and promote healing.
Barks: They are used to prepare a decoction that helps to reduce gum inflammation, ease pain, and promote healing due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier or cultivate your own.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 50 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the herbal mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with gum pain because they contain tea tree oil, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and soothes irritated gum tissue. The antiseptic properties of tea tree oil combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infection and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties ease pain and inflammation, promoting healing and relaxation of the affected area. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia capsules may help alleviate gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent to reduce gum pain and inflammation.
Linalool: Exhibits antiseptic and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce gum infection and promote a sense of calm, alleviating gum pain.
α-Terpineol: Shows anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce gum inflammation and pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Melaleuca alternifolia oil, which is used to create capsules for gum pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Stems: Stems are also a source of Melaleuca alternifolia oil, which is extracted and used in capsules for gum pain to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause pain.
Barks: Barks are another part of the plant that contains oil, which is used in capsules to help alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted supplier within 30 days of opening package.
Grind 20 grams of the dried leaves using a coffee grinder to achieve a fine powder in 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered leaves with 10 grams of cellulose powder in a clean glass container within 5 minutes.
Fill each capsule shell with approximately 0.5 gram of the powder mixture using a small spoon in 10 minutes.
Seal the capsule shells using a capsule sealer or a small brush and press in 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with gum pain because they contain quinine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory properties. Quinine has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation. The capsules may also contain other herbs that help to fight off underlying infections, which can contribute to gum pain. By addressing the root causes of the pain, Cinchona officinalis capsules may provide relief from gum discomfort, promoting a healthy and pain-free oral environment. This natural approach may also help to prevent further inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps reduce inflammation and ease pain in gums by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent.
Quinidine: Exhibits local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help numb the pain and reduce swelling in the gums.

Alkaloids (including cinchonine and cinchonidine): Possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection in gum tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids present in the leaves, such as quinine and cinchonine, help relieve pain and inflammation in the gums.
Barks: The bark of Cinchona officinalis contains compounds like quinidine and quinine, which are used to numb the gums and reduce pain.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Cinchona officinalis contain some alkaloids that contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory properties and pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Crush 30 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powder with 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl and mix well until well combined.
Add 50 milliliters of water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved in 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a capsule machine and fill 50 capsules with the herbal mixture in 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum capsules helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound, Eugenol, found in these capsules, has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums and mouth. Its ability to numb the affected area also provides temporary relief from pain, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and sensitivity. This natural solution promotes healthy gum tissue and reduces the risk of further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Eugenol: As a phenolic compound, eugenol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
α-Selinene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from damage and reduce pain associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used to make capsules for gum pain because they contain a compound called eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for gum pain because they contain eugenol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Fruits: The fruits are used to make capsules for gum pain because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum powder using a digital scale with precision.
Mix 250 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum powder with 100 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl.
Add 25 grams of glycerin and 25 grams of silica to the bowl with the powder mixture.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill empty capsules with 400 milligrams of the powder mixture.
Seal the capsules and store them in an airtight container in a cool dry place.
